title: Marsdenia lushaiensis
intro: Marsdenia lushaiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as a type of milkweed vine or moonflower. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its extracts have been found to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer properties. The bioactive constituents of Marsdenia lushaiensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Marsdenia lushaiensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This genus is a group of evergreen vines native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The plant is known to have medicinal and ornamental value. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Marsdenia lushaiensis is closely related to other plants with similar characteristics, such as milkweeds and oleanders. Further classification of Marsdenia lushaiensis is also considered as a part of the Asclepiadoideae subfamily.
uses: Marsdenia lushaiensis has been traditionally used in various medicinal practices, primarily in the northeastern parts of India.
